rulings.Case Title: RESTORATION MANAGEMENT COMPANY VS. JAMES LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Bifurcate And Try Equitable Issues Before Legal Issues At Trial
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT BRIXTON CP CHINATOWN LLC AND CF RFP SELLER LLC'S MOTION TO BIFURCATE AND TRY EQUITABLE ISSUES BEFORE LEGAL ISSUES AT TRIAL is OFF CALENDAR per request of the moving party. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VISNU PITIYANUVATH ET AL VS. ALICIA JOHNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Visnu Pitiyanuvath?S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No. One
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET NO. ONE IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: (1) DEFENDANT'S BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF CLAIM OF ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE, TO EACH SPECIFIC REQUEST OF PRODUCTION ARE OVERRULED. (2) DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO SERVE, WITHIN TWENTY 20 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO EACH REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS INCLUDING THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS WHICH ARE RESPONSIVE TO EACH PARTICULAR REQUESTS. RESPONDING GENERALLY TO ALL THE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED IS NOT CODE COMPLIANT. (3) TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT AS TO ANY SPECIFIC REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION ON A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE, DEFENDANT MUST IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENT WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ENABLE PLAINTIFF TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

DEFENDANT ALICIA JOHNSON'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part.
